title: doc: explain soft-delete vs hard-delete state in MessageUpdateInput
description: MessageUpdateInput.deleted_for_all doc (line 198-199 in methods/mod.rs) is unclear about the dual-state requirement. Currently: 'When Some(true) and deleted_at is also set, deletes for all participants...'. Clarify: 'Setting deleted_at alone marks a soft-delete (hidden from caller only). To perform a hard-delete (propagated to all participants), set BOTH deleted_at and deleted_for_all: Some(true). The server sends Peer/retract to other users.' This prevents callers from thinking deleted_for_all alone triggers hard-delete.
close_reason: Design decision: soft/hard delete semantics documented in MessageUpdateInput doc comment
